purpose: Central IPC bridge — registers ~140 ipcMain.handle() endpoints that adapt Synapse's pre-existing server services (originally HTTP/SSE) to Electron's IPC. Replaces the standalone SSE server inside the desktop app. registerIPCHandlers(getMainWindow) is called once from main.js on app.whenReady and: (1) ensures dashboards/, archive/, history/, queue/ directories exist and creates a default 'dashboard1' if none exist, (2) registers handlers grouped by domain — dashboard CRUD, settings, project, task editor, workers (Claude/Codex), terminals, commands, swarm orchestration, conversations, attachments, IDE filesystem, IDE search (ripgrep with Node fallback), IDE diagnostics (JSON via JSON.parse, JS/JSX via vm.compileFunction, CSS via hand-rolled bracket matcher), Node.js debug (CDP), Git operations (execFile-based), Live Preview, and Chat dashboard, (3) starts file watchers via WatcherService that push real-time events through createBroadcastFn — the broadcast bridge intercepts dashboards_changed events to inject persisted ordering+names from settings.dashboardMeta, sends to renderer via webContents.send, AND feeds agent_progress events to SwarmOrchestrator.handleProgressUpdate to drive the dispatch loop. Also exports a 'get-chat-system-prompt' handler that constructs the multi-section prompt for in-app chat agents (response priority protocol, communication transparency protocol, directory references with dashboard binding markers, Synapse CLAUDE.md, project CLAUDE.md). Mirrors the original src/server SSE startup but via IPC instead of HTTP/EventSource.
gotchas: createBroadcastFn intercepts 'dashboards_changed' and 'dashboards_list' events to overlay persisted ordering+names from settings.dashboardMeta — workers writing dashboards directly will not see custom display names unless they go through this bridge
The 'ide' dashboard is HARD-PINNED at index 0 in getOrderedDashboards() — reorder-dashboards filters it out then unshifts it back. Dropping this invariant breaks IDE workspace tabs

agent_progress broadcasts trigger SwarmOrchestrator.handleProgressUpdate(dashboardId, task_id, data) — this is the dispatch feedback loop. If SwarmOrchestrator isn't loaded yet, MODULE_NOT_FOUND is silently swallowed but other errors warn. Renaming the channel name 'agent_progress' breaks the orchestration loop
All ipc channel names must match the strings in preload.js's PUSH_CHANNELS whitelist OR the contextBridge method names — there is no shared constant

spawnWorker validates differently for chatMode (looks for Chat/chat{N}/agent{HEX} folders with 4-hex IDs) vs dashboardId mode (looks for dashboards/{id}) — calling without either throws no error here but downstream services may misbehave
Chat agents use 4-hex IDs; code agents use 6-hex IDs — these namespaces must not collide. create-chat-agent allocates by checking ALL existing chat dirs (cross-chat uniqueness)
ide-* handlers validate paths via ideValidatePath which prevents directory traversal — but only if workspaceRoot is provided. Missing workspaceRoot bypasses the check
ide-create-file deliberately rejects existing files with 'File already exists' — there is no overwrite flag
ide-search prefers ripgrep (rg) on PATH but falls back to a Node walker only when ENOENT/'not found' is detected in stderr — other rg failure modes return error to the renderer
Git diff/status/log uses execFile (not shell exec) — args are passed as arrays specifically to prevent shell injection from filenames
git-graph uses \x01 (SOH) as field separator because git data may legitimately contain commas/pipes
git-status uses --porcelain -uall and parses the two-character status code (X=index, Y=worktree) — the substring(3) skips the 2-char code + 1 space
git-discard-file checks for untracked files (??) and unlinks them via fs — for tracked files it does git checkout HEAD -- file. Both are destructive with no confirmation here
stopWatchers() must be called on window-all-closed (in main.js) to release fs.watch handles AND the chat dashboard recursive watcher — otherwise the process hangs

The chat dashboard watcher uses fs.watch with recursive:true which is unsupported on Linux — it will throw on Linux but is caught and logged, so the chat_dashboard_changed event silently never fires there
saveTempImages and save-temp-file write to os.tmpdir() — these files are NOT cleaned up by Synapse and accumulate; rely on OS temp cleanup
get-chat-system-prompt requires a non-null dashboardId — it throws synchronously if missing because every chat agent must be bound to a dashboard for swarm dispatch isolation
ide-check-syntax for 'typescript' uses vm.compileFunction which only validates JS-level syntax (TS types are not checked) — type errors are silently passed
The CSS diagnostic is hand-rolled bracket matching — it does not validate CSS semantics (e.g. unknown properties, invalid values), only structural balance
ProjectService is loaded lazily inside registerIPCHandlers via require — circular deps with the rest of the electron services would surface here
saveAdditionalContext stores per-dashboard arrays inside settings.dashboardAdditionalContext as a fallback for renderer localStorage; clearing settings via reset-settings purges these too
patterns: Broadcast bridge pattern: createBroadcastFn returns a closure that mirrors the SSE broadcast signature so WatcherService is reused unchanged — only the transport differs (webContents.send vs SSE)
Service init via broadcastFn injection: ClaudeCodeService.init(broadcastFn), CodexService.init(broadcastFn), etc. — services emit progress through this single channel

Persisted ordering reconciliation: getOrderedDashboards filters meta.order against existing IDs, appends new ones by creation time, ensures 'ide' first, then writes back the reconciled order — self-healing across filesystem changes
Dashboard status derivation mirrors src/server/routes/apiRoutes.js (deriveDashboardStatus duplicated) — keeping IPC and HTTP responses identical when both modes are used
Reverse-sorted match application in ide-search-replace (sort by line desc, column desc) so earlier replacements don't shift positions of later ones
Chat agent allocation: 4-hex random with up to 1000 retries, uniqueness scoped across ALL chat dirs (not per-chat), folder layout Chat/chat{N}/agent{XXXX}/
git operations use execFile + array args (never shell concat) for injection safety; all wrapped in gitExec() with consistent { success, data | error } envelope
Diagnostics multi-language dispatch via ideDiagLanguage(filePath) -> {ideDiagJSON, ideDiagJS, ideDiagCSS}

Initial-data burst pattern (sendInitialData): on window-ready, push dashboards_list + initialization + all_progress + queue_changed for every dashboard — mirrors what an SSE client would receive on /events connect
conventions: Handlers grouped by domain with banner comments (--- Section Name ---)
Each ipcMain.handle uses async () => and returns a plain object (success/data/error envelope for git+ide; raw shape for dashboard reads)
DEFAULT_INITIALIZATION/DEFAULT_LOGS spread into a fresh object on every read so callers can mutate without polluting the constant
Settings reloaded inline via require('./settings') in some handlers — leverages Node's module cache

The IDE_DEFAULT_IGNORE list ['.git', 'node_modules', '.DS_Store', '__pycache__', '.next', '.cache', 'dist', 'build', '.venv', 'venv'] is the single source of truth for both ide-read-dir and ide-search
